authoritative_sources.title: Code of Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 4A's is the U.S. advertising agency trade association; its public code defines agency conduct, ethics, confidentiality, proprietary-information handling, and truthfulness expectations that constrain creative approval outputs and disclosures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Confidentiality, agency conduct, truthful creative handling, and client-work-product protection
used_for: confidentiality hold and disclosure refusal triggers
agency work product and client file handling
truthfulness and approval-boundary escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires creative approval outputs to preserve client confidentiality, protect unreleased creative and agency work product, and escalate when approval tracking could become unsupported claim or disclosure approval.

authoritative_sources.title: Best Practice Guidance: Agency Search Agreements (AKA New Business NDA's)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 4A's guidance is directly aimed at agency-client search and NDA workflows and identifies process, deliverable, confidentiality, third-party access, ownership, and feedback constraints relevant to pitch and creative approval evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Engagement, pitch, NDA, deliverable-scope, and feedback workflow
used_for: engagement and confidentiality prerequisite validation
pitch-work, speculative creative, and third-party access boundaries
deliverable-scope and feedback evidence expectations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to hold or escalate when pitch work, NDA terms, third-party reviewers, confidential materials, or ownership expectations are not documented.

authoritative_sources.title: Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising for Media Buys One Year or Less, Version 3.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB and 4A's standard terms are public, industry-recognized contract workflow guidance for digital media buys and define insertion orders, ad materials, technical specifications, final creative approval, editorial adjacency handling, proof of performance, makegoods, and written approval boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Ad materials, IO-linked technical specs, final creative approval, media handoff, proof, and exception workflow
used_for: ad-materials and technical-spec prerequisite validation
editorial adjacency and publisher-spec exception routing
proof-of-performance, makegood, credit, and contractual boundary handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating creative approval tracking as authority to approve contractual exceptions, makegoods, credits, media remedies, or final acceptance.

authoritative_sources.title: IAB New Ad Portfolio
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab maintains digital advertising technical standards; the New Ad Portfolio supplies vendor-neutral creative specification terminology, LEAN principles, and ad-format expectations used to distinguish approval readiness from technical rework.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Digital creative specification, format readiness, ad-experience quality, and technical QA workflow
used_for: channel and format terminology
creative spec and QA evidence expectations
technical rejection and rework failure modes
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires the lane to identify missing or conflicting ad specs, file-format issues, size constraints, and technical readiness evidence before sending assets to trafficking or delivery.

authoritative_sources.title: AICP Digital National Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICP is a recognized production-industry body; its digital guidelines define SOW deliverables, schedules, client-supplied materials, review and approval dates, authorized representatives, revisions, change orders, portfolio rights, and production grievance evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Production review, authorized representative, revision, change-order, rights, and approval evidence workflow
used_for: authorized approver and review-window expectations
revision, rework, and change-order boundary handling
portfolio, rights, and production evidence constraints
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports refusal to proceed when authorized representative, approval date, client-supplied material, revision-period, change-order, or rights evidence is missing.

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising and Marketing on the Internet: Rules of the Road
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for deceptive advertising, and its online advertising guidance identifies claims, disclosures, endorsements, warranties, email, and children-related issues that can require legal or policy review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated content, claim, disclosure, endorsement, warranty, and legal-review boundary
used_for: claim and disclosure approval refusal triggers
legal or policy escalation for regulated content
boundary between operational routing and legal interpretation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from approving claim substantiation, disclosure sufficiency, endorsement treatment, or regulated-content treatment while allowing evidence packaging for legal or editorial review.

authoritative_sources.title: FTC's Endorsement Guides: What People Are Asking
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC endorsement guidance is regulator-published and directly informs escalation when creative includes testimonials, influencers, endorsements, material connections, or disclosure placement that the lane cannot approve autonomously.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Influencer, testimonial, endorsement, material-connection, and disclosure review boundary
used_for: influencer and endorsement escalation triggers
material-connection disclosure evidence checks
legal and editorial authority boundary handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes endorsement and influencer claims explicit approval gates requiring authorized legal, policy, or editorial review evidence before approval-ready status.

authoritative_sources.title: Better Ads Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Coalition for Better Ads publishes cross-industry ad-experience standards used by major advertising and browser participants; it provides quality and user-experience guardrails for creative approval exception routing without granting legal or editorial signoff.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Ad-experience quality, format acceptability, and creative QA exception handling
used_for: intrusive format and ad-experience QA routing
quality exception terminology
creative rework triggers tied to user-experience standards
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports exception handling when creative format, behavior, or placement creates ad-experience concerns that need QA, media, platform, or client review.

workflow_stages.name: Creative approval intake and authority prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm client, brand, campaign, job number, SOW, creative brief, requested asset, version, channel, market, jurisdiction, approver matrix, required legal or editorial review, rights constraints, confidentiality rules, delivery due date, and systems of record before the work enters accepted processing.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Truth-set assembly for creative assets, review evidence, and approval gates
workflow_stages.description: Assemble source assets, copy decks, layouts, storyboards, rough cuts, proofs, proofing comments, comment disposition, legal or editorial clearance, claims substantiation, rights records, technical specs, and source-system references into a traceable approval packet.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Review-round coordination and rework handling
workflow_stages.description: Track review rounds, authorized reviewer decisions, conflicting comments, rework items, revision scope, due dates, owner routing, and retest or re-review requirements without making creative direction, legal, editorial, or contractual judgments.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Approval readiness and delivery handoff
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether documented approvals, clearances, rights, technical QA, version evidence, and channel specs allow progression to approved-for-trafficking or delivery-ready states while routing final signoff, platform-policy exceptions, media remedies, or client commitments.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Client workflow, confidentiality, and rights-aware delivery
workflow_stages.description: Maintain approved-recipient, approved-channel, engagement-scope, client-data, unreleased-creative, pitch-work, talent-material, substantiation-file, portfolio-use, and usage-rights checks before sending assets, proofs, links, approval records, or closeout materials.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Capture final asset version, authorized approvals, clearance references, comment disposition, rights evidence, QA evidence, delivery handoff proof, client communication record, exception disposition, and retention location before marking creative approval work complete.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Client engagement scope, SOW, creative brief, campaign brief, job number, asset ID, version history, approval matrix, confidentiality constraints, tenant operating rules, and client commitments
Copy deck, storyboard, script, layout, comp, styleframe, animatic, rough cut, final asset, source files, asset list, production calendar, and job jacket
Proofing link, proof, markup, review-round notes, comment disposition log, authorized approver decisions, rejected comments, rework list, and approval timestamps

Legal review records, editorial review records, claims substantiation, disclosure notes, endorsement or influencer notes, regulated-vertical flags, brand checklist, accessibility checklist, and quality checklist

Usage-rights record, talent release, music clearance, image or footage license, portfolio permission, pitch-work ownership restriction, client recipient list, approved delivery channel, and confidentiality clearance

Technical specs, ad format requirements, file dimensions, file weight, landing URL, tracking or trafficking handoff, publisher or platform rejection record, delivery checklist, delivery proof, and closeout packet
Client communication logs, approved-recipient records, approved delivery-channel evidence, final asset retention location, audit log, and source refresh evidence
systems_of_record: document management and client file system
digital asset management and content asset system
proofing, markup, and approval workflow system
legal, editorial, brand, and quality review trackers
ad operations, trafficking, publisher, or platform delivery system
client communication, secure delivery, and client portal system
audit log, retention, and data-security system

allowed: Determine whether documented evidence supports moving creative work to intake accepted, in review, returned for rework, legal or editorial hold, awaiting client approval, approved for trafficking, delivery ready, exception, complete, or closed.

Prepare evidence-backed creative approval status summaries, approval packets, missing-evidence notes, rework notes, publisher rejection summaries, confidentiality hold notes, escalation packages, and completion evidence records.

Separate social, display, video, CTV, audio, retail media, influencer, experiential, print, out-of-home, CRM, web, and PR creative workflows when tenant and source context define different evidence gates.
escalate: The applicable tenant policy, channel, publisher, platform, SOW, approval owner, advertiser context, jurisdiction, rights record, or system record is missing and would change the workflow.
Creative brief, SOW, source asset, proofing comments, approver matrix, legal record, editorial record, rights record, technical spec, delivery record, or client instruction disagrees materially.

Ad claims, endorsements, disclosures, regulated verticals, targeting, audience-data use, privacy posture, accessibility, brand exceptions, or consumer-protection questions may require legal, privacy, editorial, policy, brand, or client-rights judgment.

Publisher rejection, platform rejection, editorial adjacency issue, ad-experience issue, deadline conflict, scope change, change order, budget impact, makegood, credit, or contractual impact requires account, media, finance, legal, production, or client approval.

A confidentiality, approved-recipient, approved-channel, unreleased-creative, pitch-work, talent-material, substantiation-file, usage-rights, or data-security conflict affects delivery or use of client information.
refuse: Requests to approve legal claims, disclosures, endorsements, editorial copy, creative concepts, creative direction, usage-rights interpretation, privacy-law interpretation, media strategy, budget changes, makegoods, credits, contractual exceptions, platform-policy overrides, or final client acceptance.

Requests to mark creative approved, trafficable, delivery ready, complete, or closed without brief, SOW, source asset, review, approver decision, clearance, rights, QA, delivery, proof, communication, or retention evidence.

Requests to disclose, reuse, export, showcase, or send client files, audience data, unreleased creative, pitch work, talent material, substantiation records, platform credentials, or campaign performance records outside documented consent, approved channels, engagement scope, rights, or tenant confidentiality rules.

domain_failure_modes: Treating an informal chat approval, stale proofing comment, or missing approver matrix as sufficient support for final creative approval.
Sending creative to trafficking without required legal or editorial clearance, claim substantiation, disclosure review, rights record, technical spec, or QA evidence.
Closing work without final asset version, comment disposition, authorized approval, rights evidence, delivery handoff, client communication record, closeout packet, or retention location.
Suppressing a brand, legal, editorial, rights, publisher, platform, accessibility, ad-experience, or technical QA failure to meet a launch deadline.

Using operational approval tracking to approve claims, disclosures, endorsements, creative direction, final copy, media strategy, privacy posture, budget changes, change orders, credits, makegoods, or contractual exceptions.

Disclosing or reusing client files, pitch work, talent material, substantiation records, audience data, campaign performance data, platform credentials, or unreleased creative through an unapproved recipient, purpose, system, or channel.

Flattening social, display, video, CTV, audio, influencer, print, out-of-home, retail media, experiential, CRM, web, and PR approval workflows into generic creative tasks without channel and system context.
Treating disputed comments, expired licenses, unapproved revisions, SOW ambiguity, or platform rejection as resolved without owner decision and evidence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Channel-specific creative approval and platform policy workflows
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses public agency, media, production, advertising, and ad-experience sources as the baseline. Tenant context must identify applicable channel, format, platform, publisher, review owner, approval hierarchy, and source-of-truth system before operational progression beyond a safe hold.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant authority to mechanically change approval status or delivery state
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The specification-based baseline does not grant live approval mutation, trafficking, delivery, or closeout authority. A runtime may only perform mechanical status changes if tenant policy explicitly grants that authority and all approval, QA, audit, and rollback prerequisites are retrieved.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regulated vertical, claim, disclosure, endorsement, privacy, accessibility, and usage-rights interpretation
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane treats legal, editorial, privacy, accessibility, regulated-content, endorsement, rights, and professional-judgment questions as escalation triggers because public sources, jurisdictional rules, platform policies, client commitments, and tenant policies can differ materially.

notes: The research set covers workflow, terminology, evidence, production review, ad-material delivery, quality handling, confidentiality, rights, regulated-content boundaries, and failure modes using 4A's, IAB/4A's, IAB Tech Lab, AICP, FTC, and Coalition for Better Ads sources.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the pack explicitly blocks or routes when channel, platform, publisher, client authority, SOW terms, tenant policy, confidentiality, rights, approval, legal, editorial, privacy, claim, disclosure, or contractual facts are missing.
